name: environmental_health_whistleblower_marrowbridge_st_lt
premise: A Environmental Health response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving environmental health whistleblower marrowbridge st lt. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before regulators arrive. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as short term vs long term under pressure. A whistleblower has already contacted media; the narrative is forming now. Constraints: Ensure a usable human override/appeals path. Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability.

agents.name: Elena Mensah
agents.identity: I am Elena Mensah, accountable for a defensible call in Environmental Health when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays long term for short term.

agents.name: Kaito Nielsen
agents.identity: I am Kaito Nielsen, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Environmental Health.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so long term isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of short term.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect long term.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to long term to secure short term under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before regulators arrive. Evidence is partial. Elena Mensah must decide under short term vs long term. What does Elena Mensah do?
